item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it's a mistake but I'm a bit hesitant, so just in case: This and SCP-4578 say they're set in 2L, but SCP-4922 says that's one of the moons. Should these be 2N like the rest?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm more disturbed by the fact that your zoo is apparently open while COVID-19 is out and about. Please only attempt further Orangutassinations remotely until the quarantine lifts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… am I a bad person for playing this on my phone, as loudly as possible, to a group of Orangutans at the local zoo to see what would happen?

They mostly just ignored it. One of them started doing inappropriate things with his hand and a certain other part of his anatomy. Damn rude monkeys

Anyway, the concept of Orangutans on fire is disturbingly amusing to me so have a +1 you twisted bastard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely adore the concept that Corbenic somehow made a weapon out of their national anthem. (and also that they actually did and thought of it lol something about that just aligns perfectly with what they are)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the implications of this article for three moons canon are… very large.

+1

item.title: nothing in the first collapsible?
item.content:encoded: I'm not seeing anything under + SCP-5319-1 Remote Access - 2/5319 Clearance Required. I'm guessing this is where the audio file should be. Is it just me?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a wonderful flavor of bizarre. Doesn't outstay its welcome, anomaly is more than original, and the correspondence with Three Moons was appropriate and fun.

Bravo. +1

item.title: Fantastic!
item.content:encoded: Superb job, conveys exactly what it needs to. I would classify the amnestics used, however.

item.title: Re: To Be Crit Soon!
item.content:encoded: Your crit is amazing, as to be expected. Thanks!

item.title: Re: To Be Crit Soon!
item.content:encoded: Perdition is a name for Hell, so…

item.title: Jr. Staff Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
Hello hello!

I’m Kara from the Site Crit team!

+ Ze Crit O’ Doom
- ...

- “The following day, all nine resident Sumatran orangutans spontaneously ignited during viewing hours.” I would like more detail to what they did after this. Did the people freak out?

- “Perdition Committee” Is this a Foundation thing? I would assume not, but I’m still a bit confused on that. What does the Perdition Committee do? I would suggest using a footnote to add that.

- In the addendum orangutan is spelled wrong.

Final thoughts:

This was a fun read. It opens a lot of doors for some tales and other things to go along with it, and I tend to like SCPs like that. I can’t help but feel there's something missing, and I can’t put my finger on it. I don’t think it's in the end since that part gives me a more “To be continued” feeling, which isn’t the issue.

As a sidebar on the music, I think it was ok. The melody got extremely repetitive and sometimes it can become stale to the listener. I didn’t exactly like the ending, but I think that's just because I’m a music snob. Other than that, great job on the composition part.

Overall, great job! I really liked this and it kept my attention to the end. I hope to see more from you in the future!

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to KaraKatt or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"When SCP-5319 is exposed, in any form, to an orangutan, the specimen will burst into flames."

This may be the single best sentence on this entire site. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Along with the generally peculiar but intriguing base anomaly here, something about the interactions between the Three Moons Initiative and the Foundation, particularly the way you made these entities feel more like local librarians than extradimensional protectors, really made this skip for me. +1.

item.title: Note about the audio file
item.content:encoded: I made it in Garageband. Also, my parakeets make tons of noise whenever I play it — seems I've got a couple of three moons spies.
